principle: Injected media, text, audio-reactive state, and compositor/drift fields must declare a deterministic mount contract before entering DarkPlaces/Quake.

mounts.name: OARB

drift_interaction.darkplaces_role: spatialized live sphere, occlusion, light reception
drift_interaction.principle: The OARB is not a passive video ball; drift/compositing gives the attention object its visible substance inside the AoA.

hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax live-media producer writes raw source frames; screwm_media_drift owns sphere-front BGRA projection when GPU projection is enabled
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot updates the model skinframe in place
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: sphere-front projection preserves declared media aspect inside a spherical OARB
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns media/projection/drift pixels; DarkPlaces owns in-world spherical placement

mounts.per_face_surface_atlas: aoa.mdl skinframe maps one triangular fractal face per atlas cell
mounts.occlusion_policy: OARB origin remains the AoA incenter and is perfectly inscribed in the regular tetrix central void; lattice opacity and face atlas must not depth-veil the live media sphere

mounts.name: AoA Face Atlas
mounts.expanded_name: Angle of Attention Fractal Face Control Atlas
mounts.purpose: One independently addressable compositor/color/expressivity control cell for every rendered non-negative AoA fractal face

drift_interaction.darkplaces_role: regular tetrix geometry, occlusion, light reception, per-face skinframe mapping
drift_interaction.principle: AoA fractal faces are geometric information surfaces; drift acts on the rendered tetrahedral faces, not as a fourth-wall overlay.

hybrid_contract.producer_binding: AoA face atlas source writes one deterministic raw control cell per rendered fractal face; screwm_media_drift owns final BGRA when GPU drift is enabled
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot 14 updates the AoA model skinframe in place
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: 2048 square atlas arranged as 32x32 cells; each rendered fractal face receives one triangular control cell
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns per-face color/compositor signals; DarkPlaces owns regular tetrix placement and depth.

mounts.face_control_schema: face_index-addressed JSON controls with one addressable 64x64 atlas cell per rendered AoA facet; controls may set rgb/color, intensity, edge_gain, stripe_gain, and stipple_gain for that face only
mounts.face_control_scope: face_index controls are independent and non-aggregate: no grouping, no bleed into adjacent facets, and no fourth-wall overlay target

mounts.purpose: Reverie material field as an in-world Scroom ward, not an atlas reprojection

drift_interaction.principle: Reverie is a live material field; atlas storage may proxy it, but the primary ward is a direct live texture.

hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax imagination renderer writes live Reverie BGRA frames
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot updates the reverie ward surface in place
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: surface height is derived from source_aspect; no atlas cell crop
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns Reverie/drift pixels; DarkPlaces owns spatialized ward placement

texture_transform.reason: Direct Reverie ward uses surface-local mapping so the live material field is not world-space tiled or atlas-cropped

mounts.purpose: spatial Hapax speech representation as a raw time-domain oscilloscope near the AoA
mounts.legibility_basis: single-stroke oscilloscope signal: 512 source pixels over 18deg yields 28.4 px/deg, above the declared line-continuity floor while preserving the task's 512x128 low-latency slot

drift_interaction.principle: Hapax speech is represented as a live time-domain waveform; amplitude shapes only the receiver line width/alpha and never a global scene pulse.

hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax speech-wave producer writes 512x128 raw BGRA frames from the daimonion speech envelope ring
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot 13 updates the speech waveform surface in place
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: surface height is derived from the 4:1 oscilloscope aspect; transparent background preserves a floating waveform
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns speech waveform pixels; DarkPlaces owns spatialized placement near the AoA

texture_transform.reason: OARB-depth y-facing waveform uses surface-local mapping so the live oscilloscope spans the receiver once without world-space tiling

mounts.purpose: operator IR ward instrument as a direct in-world live texture

drift_interaction.darkplaces_role: spatialized IR receiver, light reception, occlusion
drift_interaction.principle: IR camera wards are live instrument surfaces in the Scroom, not hidden atlas proxies or stale homage placeholders.

hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax IR camera producer writes raw BGRA frames; screwm_media_drift owns final ward BGRA when GPU drift is enabled
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot 15 updates ward 18 in place
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: surface is square greyscale IR; no atlas cell crop
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns IR camera pixels and drift; DarkPlaces owns spatialized ward placement

texture_transform.reason: Left-wall x-facing IR ward uses surface-local mapping so the direct camera frame spans ward 18 once without atlas reprojection

hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns IR camera pixels and drift; DarkPlaces owns spatialized ward placement

texture_transform.reason: Left-wall x-facing IR ward uses surface-local mapping so the direct camera frame spans ward 19 once without atlas reprojection

drift_interaction.principle: Camera wards are shaped by mediated presence and drift, not by a physical frame.

hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax camera producer writes raw BGRA frames
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot updates the BSP texture in place
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: surface height is derived from source_aspect; no arbitrary crop
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns camera pixels; DarkPlaces owns spatialized instrument placement

texture_transform.reason: Left-wall x-facing inward BSP face must use surface-local UV origin; otherwise world-space coordinates wrap the live camera frame

drift_interaction.principle: Ticker wards are live operational text fields whose apparent form comes from text cadence plus drift.

hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax Cairo/Pango ticker producer writes raw BGRA frames
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot updates the ward surface in place
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: surface height is derived from source_aspect; text is ellipsized or scrolled by producer
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns text layout and drift pixels; DarkPlaces owns the walkable spatial mount

texture_transform.reason: Quake y-facing BSP text basis requires surface-local origin plus 180-degree rotation; vertical precompensation belongs to the producer

producer_pretransform.reason: Mount-space ticker pixels are vertically preflipped so the stable DarkPlaces BSP mapping reads upright

mounts.purpose: primary compositor-authored pixel layer for all in-world Scroom wards

drift_interaction.darkplaces_role: per-ward spatial receiver, light reception, occlusion
drift_interaction.principle: Atlas storage never defines a ward's visible shape; each ward receives visible substance through its source and drift relation.

hybrid_contract.quake_binding: BSP brush texture ward_atlas with per-ward UV cell mapping
hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax Cairo/shared-memory ward atlas producer renders compositor ward sources into deterministic cells
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: DarkPlaces live-texture slot updates one atlas; each ward mount samples its declared cell
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: atlas cells are storage only; each visible mount derives aspect from its source, purpose, and intended inspection distance
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns ward pixels and deep Homage expression; DarkPlaces owns walkable spatial placement, occlusion, and lighting
